data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Morgan Stanley beats Q1 estimates; Institutional Securities hits $10.7B record revenue
Asml narrows 2026 sales outlook to €36B-€40B while targeting at least 80 low na euv units in 2027
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) Q1 2026

Management view

  • “For the quarter, total net sales came in at EUR 8.8 billion. That was within guidance.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board R.J.M. Dassen)

  • “Included in the EUR 8.8 billion was EUR 2.5 billion for Installed Base revenue. That was a little bit above the guidance.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “If you look at the gross margin for Q1, 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “Net income for the quarter, EUR 2.8 billion.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “We see that the semiconductor industry growth continued to solidify. This is still very much driven by investment in AI infrastructure.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Christophe Fouquet)

  • “We expect, in fact, that the supply will not meet the demand for the foreseeable future.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

  • “What our customers tell us is that they are sold out for 2026 and their supply constraint will last beyond 2026.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

  • “We believe we can drive an output for this year of at least 60 systems for EUV Low NA.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

Outlook

  • “For Q2, we expect EUR 8.4 billion to EUR 9 billion of total net sales.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “We expect the gross margin to be between 51% and 52%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “We are actually narrowing the window and also increasing the window of our expectation to EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion for this year.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “For the gross margin, we maintain our expectation of 51% to 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Compared with Q4 2025 guidance language, management moved from “EUR 34 billion and EUR 39 billion” for 2026 to “EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion,” while keeping the 2026 gross margin range at “51% to 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • The provided analystsEstimates data was not used because it did not include a valid fiscalQuarter format (Q1–Q4) as required.

Financial results

  • “For the quarter, total net sales came in at EUR 8.8 billion.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “Included in the EUR 8.8 billion was EUR 2.5 billion for Installed Base revenue.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “If you look at the gross margin for Q1, 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • “Net income for the quarter, EUR 2.8 billion.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Management tied the quarter’s margin to mix within Installed Base: “there were components in there that actually come in at quite some strong gross margins. So as a result of that, a pretty high gross margin at 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

Q&A

  • No analyst Q&A session was included in this transcript; the discussion was a company-led video format with prompts from Investor Relations.

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts sentiment could not be assessed because no analysts participated in the transcript.

  • Management tone was positive and capacity-focused in both prepared remarks and the IR-led exchanges, with assertive demand language such as “sold out for 2026” and “supply will not meet the demand for the foreseeable future.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

  • Versus the prior quarter’s tone that “the market outlook has notably improved in the last few months,” the current call reinforced that improvement with more specific constraint language around 2026 and beyond. (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Guidance shifted upward and narrowed: Q4 2025 included “EUR 34 billion and EUR 39 billion” for 2026 revenue, while Q1 2026 updated to “EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion,” with the 2026 gross margin expectation unchanged at “51% to 53%.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Non-EUV expectations changed from “kind of flattish” (Q4 2025) to “an increase of demand” and “increased revenue on the non-EUV business” (Q1 2026), with Dassen citing immersion demand having “reversed itself.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Installed Base commentary remained oriented around upgrades and fast capacity adds; Q1 2026 emphasized this as “a very fast way for our customers to increase their capacity.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Technology roadmap updates became more specific on throughput and long-term extendability, including: “in 2031, we'll be able to run this tool at 330 wafers per hour,” and near-term NXE:3800E throughput moving “from 220 to 230 wafers per hour.” (President, CEO and Chair of the Board of Management Fouquet)

Risks and concerns

  • Export controls were flagged as an uncertainty within full-year planning: “within the guidance that we provided, the EUR 36 billion to EUR 40 billion, we believe we can accommodate potential outcomes of the export control discussions that are currently ongoing.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

  • Execution risk around demand reversals and ramp timing was implied in immersion: “we actually had a bit of a slow start… That has now reversed itself,” paired with the mitigation message that ASML still expects “pretty close to the immersion sales that we had last year in terms of unit numbers.” (Executive VP, CFO & Member of the Management Board Dassen)

Final takeaway

Management presented Q1 2026 as within its sales guidance while attributing margin strength to Installed Base mix, then raised and narrowed full-year 2026 net sales expectations to €36 billion–€40 billion while maintaining a 51%–53% gross margin range. Leadership framed demand as being driven by AI infrastructure and described customers as “sold out for 2026,” with capacity expansion plans supported by ASML targets of at least 60 Low NA EUV systems in 2026 and at least 80 in 2027 if demand supports it, alongside roadmap throughput gains and a stated ability to absorb potential export-control outcomes within the updated 2026 range.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: GXO Logistics extends French logistics partnership with Electro Dépôt
data.description:
• GXO Logistics (GXO) extended its long-standing logistics partnership with Electro Dépôt in France.
  • The agreement builds on an existing relationship, with GXO (GXO) continuing to manage warehousing and distribution operations for Electro Dépôt’s retail network across France.
  • The partnership is expected to leverage GXO’s automation and operational capabilities to improve efficiency, inventory management, and order fulfillment, supporting Electro Dépôt’s growth strategy in the competitive consumer electronics market.

data.title: Trump says he will fire Fed's Powell if he doesn't leave 'on time'
data.description:
President Donald Trump said he would move to fire Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell if he does not step down from the Board of Governors “on time,” escalating tensions with the central bank.

Trump also said he is not planning to drop a Department of Justice probe into Powell and reiterated the need to investigate issues tied to the Federal Reserve’s building project.

“We have to find out what happened,” he told Fox Business, while adding that he hopes Kevin Warsh will be confirmed as the next Fed chairman. 

The comments come after Powell said in March that he does not plan to leave his position on the Board of Governors until a Justice investigation involving him has concluded.

Under the Federal Reserve Act, a president can remove a member of the Board of Governors "for cause," a standard that is not clearly defined.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Morgan Stanley GAAP EPS of $3.43 beats by $0.41, revenue of $20.58B beats by $820M
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley press release (MS): Q1 GAAP EPS of $3.43 beats by $0.41.
  • Revenue of $20.58B (+16.0% Y/Y) beats by $820M.
  • The Standardized Common Equity Tier 1 capitalratio was 15.1%.
  • Investment Management results reflect netrevenues of $1.5 billion, primarily driven by assetmanagement fees on higher average AUM. Thequarter included positive long-term net flows of $3.3billion
  • Shares +0.7% PM.

data.title: Parsons JV lands $25M contract for Coast Guard modernization plan
Aurora Cannabis acquires Safari Flower Company for C$26.5M
data.description:
Aurora Cannabis (ACB) has acquired Safari Flower Company for about C$26.5M, including up to C$2M in contingent cash payments.

As consideration on closing, Aurora issued the selling shareholder 2,417,180 common shares and paid the selling shareholder C$15M in cash, subject to customary adjustments post-closing.

Safari Flower’s 59,000-square-foot EU GMP-certified indoor cultivation and manufacturing facility in Ontario, Canada, will provide Aurora Cannabis (ACB) with incremental capacity that is closely aligned with its existing cultivation and manufacturing sites.

The increased capacity will be used to supply EU GMP flower to Aurora's key international markets, including Germany, Australia, Poland, and the UK, and support further market expansion.

This transaction is expected to deliver positive adjusted EBITDA contributions in fiscal year 2027, with incremental benefits in fiscal year 2028 and beyond.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Mortgage demand rises as rates fall amid the evolving situation in the Middle East
data.description:
• MBA Mortgage Applications
  • Composite Index: +1.8% to 281.0, compared to -0.8% to 276.0 in the prior week.
  • Purchase Index: -1.0% to 159.5, vs. +1.1% to 161.1 a week ago.
  • Refinance Index: +5.1% to 966.8, vs. -2.8% to 919.9 in the prior week.
  • 30-year fixed-rate mortgages came in at 6.42%, compared to 6.51% last week.
  • "Given the evolving situation in the Middle East and its impact on energy and commodity prices, mortgage rates declined last week," said Mortgage Bankers Association Economist Joel Kan.
  • "Purchase activity remained subdued as potential homebuyers remained hesitant given the current economic uncertainty, which kept purchase applications below last year's level for the second consecutive week," said Kan.

data.title: Stereotaxis to acquire Robocath for $20M upfront
data.description:
Stereotaxis (STXS) has agreed to acquire France-based Robocath, a venture-backed developer of robotic technologies for interventional cardiology and neurointerventions.

The transaction consideration includes an upfront payment of $20M and additional contingent payments of up to $25M tied to regulatory and commercial milestones, including FDA clearance of Robocath’s next-generation system.

The deal is subject to customary closing conditions and is expected to close in mid-2026. Upon closing, Robocath will operate as a wholly owned subsidiary of Stereotaxis.

This acquisition significantly strengthens Stereotaxis’ position as a platform for the full spectrum of endovascular procedures. Robocath’s flagship R-One+ system is said to be the only commercially available robotic solution currently available for percutaneous coronary interventions in Europe.

Stereotaxis plans to accelerate the development of Robocath’s next-generation system and pursue regulatory submissions in the United States and Europe within the next two years.

Robocath is estimated to contribute approximately $2M in annual revenue during the first year post-acquisition. The acquisition is expected to become breakeven by the third year post-acquisition, supported by commercial and operational synergies.

data.title: Bank of America in charts: Consumer banking revenue +5% Y/Y, global wealth and investment management +11.5%
